source_metadata.title_raw: Gary L. Edwards - A-18-011 - February 7, 2018 - Sonoma

content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacrnmento, CA 958 t 1 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Gary L. Edwards 
Councilmember, City of Sonoma 
No. I The Plaza 
Sonoma, CA 95476 
Re: 
Your Request for Ad~ice 
Our File No. A-18-011 
Dear Mr. Edwards: 
February 7, 2018 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict of interest provisions of 
the Political Reform Act (the "Act"). 1 Please note that we are only providing advice under the 
conflict of interest provisions of the Act and not under other general conflict ofinterest prohibitions 
such as common law conflict of interest or Section 1090. Also, note that we are not a finder of fact 
when rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71), and any advice we provide assumes 
your facts are complete and accurate. If this is not the case or if the facts underlying these decisions 
should change, you should contact us for additional advice. 
QUESTION 
Under the Act: may you participate in the City Council's decisions concerning the removal 
or maintenance of the trees lining Broadway, given that your family trust owns property on a 
neighboring block and receives lease payments from the mortgage firm that leases the property? 
CONCLUSION 
Yes. You may participate in those decisions because they would not result in a reasonably 
foreseeable material financial effect on any of your interests based on the facts presented. 
FACTS 
In 1992, the City of Sonoma planted 38 trees along Broadway. Some property owners and 
tenants of commercial prope1ties adjoining the tree-lined areas now assert that the trees and their 
roots are causing problems, including interference with sewer laterals, sidewalk uplift, and damage 
to building foundations. The property owners and tenants have requested to have the trees removed. 
As of the date of your request for advice, whether the trees and their root systems are the cause of 
the claimed damages remains unclear, and data is still being gathered. 
1 The Political Refonn Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 


File No. A-18-011 
Page No. 2 
You are a Councilmember for the City of Sonoma. The City Council may consider several 
options for the trees including the removal or replacement of some or all of the trees, or funding 
maintenance to prevent or minimize the reported problems. 
Your family trust has a 100-percent ownership interest in real property located in the City of 
Sonoma {the "Property"). The Property, a 700-square-foot building with one parking space 
designated solely for disabled persons, is currently leased to a mortgage firm under a five-year 
lease, with the option to extend the lease by an additional five years. Currently, the monthly lease 
payment is $2,450. The Property is located approximately 300-feet away from the portion of 
Broadway to be remediated, on a parallel city block. 
Your wife and your children are trustees and lifetime beneficiaries of the trust. In a 
telephone conversation on January 29, 2018, you noted that you and your wife created the trust and 
that you have the ability, in coordination with your fellow trustees, to amend the trust or change 
trustees or beneficiaries of the trust. Your interest in the trust has a value exceeding $2,000. 
ANALYSIS 
Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in making, or using 
his or her position to influence a governmental decision in which the official has a financial interest. 
Under Section 87103, interests from which a conflict of interest may arise include: 
• 
An interest in any business entity in which the official has a direct or indirect investment2 
worth $2,000 or more. {Section 87103{a).) 
• 
An interest in any source of income aggregating $500 or more to the official within 12 
months prior to the decision. (Section 87103( c ). ) 
• 
An interest in any business entity in which the public official is a director, officer, partner, 
trustee, employee, or holds any position of management. {Section 87l03{d).) 
• 
An interest in your personal finances and those of immediate family members. (See Section 
87103.) An official always has an interest in his or her personal finances. 
The trust itself is operated as a property management business, which qualifies as a 
"business entity" under Section 82005, and you have an investment in the trust worth $2,000 or 
more. Therefore, you have an interest in the trust as a business entity. 
Additionally, Regulation 18234(a) provides that an official has an interest in the pro rata 
share of the interest in real property, sources of income, and investments of a trust in which the 
official has a direct, indirect, or beneficial interest of 10-percent or greater. An official has a direct, 
2 Section 87103 provides: "For purposes of this section, indirect investment or interest means any investment 
or interest owned by the spouse or dependent child of a public official, by an agent on behalf of a public official, or by a 
business entity or trust in which the official, the official's agents, spouse, and dependent children own directly, 
indirectly, or beneficially a JO-percent interest or greater." 


File No. A-18-011 
Page No. 3 
indirect, or beneficial interest in real property, sources of income, and investments of a trust if the 
official 1s: 
"( 1) A trustor and: 
"(A) Can revoke or terminate the trust; 
"(B) Has retained or reserved any rights to the income or principal of the trust, or 
retained any reversionary or remainder interest; or 
"(C) Has retained or reserved any power of appointment, including but not limited to 
the power to change the trustee, or the power to amend, alter or designate, either 
alone or in conjunction with anyone else, the person or persons who shall possess or 
enjoy the trust property or income." (Regulation l 8234(c).) 
You and your wife are the trustors of the trust and have reserved some power to change 
trustees and beneficiaries. Thus, you have an interest in real property, sources of income, and 
investments of the trust including the Property and the mortgage finn, assuming your pro rata share 
of the rent paid by the mortgage finn to the trust aggregates $500 or more in the 12 months prior to 
the decisions at issue. 
Based upon the facts provided, you have interests in: (1) the trust as a business entity; (2) 
the Property; (3) the mortgage firm as a source of income; and (4) your personal finances and those 
of your immediate family members. 3 
Foreseeability a11d Materiality 
The standard for foreseeability varies depending on whether an interest is explicitly 
involved in the decision.4 For a financial interest that is not explicitly involved in the decision at 
issue, the financial effect of the decision on an official's interest is reasonably foreseeable if it can 
be recognized as a realistic possibility and more than hypothetical or theoretical. (Regulation 
1870l{b).) Your interests are not explicitly involved in the decisions at issue. 
The Property 
Generally, an effect on property not explicitly involved in the decision is material if the 
decision may foreseeably influence the market value of the property. (Regulation 18702.2(a)(l2).) 
Under Regulation 18702.2( c )(1 ), a materiality exception applies where a decision solely concerns 
3 An effect on an official's interest in his or her personal finances or those of immediate family members is not 
considered separately if the decision also affects the official's interest in a business entity or real property (Regulation 
18702.S(c).) Because the decisions at issue also affect your interest in the trust and the Property, we do not further 
analyze your interest in your personal finances. 
4 An official's financial interest is explicitly involved in a governmental decision if the interest is a named 
party in, or subject of, the decision. A financial interest is the subject of a proceeding if the decision involves the 
issuance, renewal, approval, denial, or revocation of any license, permit, or other entitlement to, or contract with, the 
financial interest, or if the decision affects a real property interest described in Regulation 18702.2(a)(l)-(6). 
(Regulation 1870 I (a).) 


File No.A-18-011 
PageNo.4 
repairs, replacement, or maintenance of existing streets, water, sewer, storm drainage, or similar 
facilities. Based on the facts you provided, decisions regarding repairs, replacement, or maintenance 
of the streets, sidewalks, and sewer laterals for damage caused by the trees, including the removal 
of trees to prevent further damage, fall under this exception. 
Trust and Mortgage Firm 
Regulation 18702.1 sets forth the materiality standards applicable to a decision's effect on 
an official's interest in a business including a business entity as a source of income. 5 Regulation 
18702.l(b) provides that a decision's effect is material if a prudent person with sufficient 
information would find it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision's effect would contribute to a 
change in the value of the privately-held business entity. In this case, the potential removal and 
maintenance of the trees is a decision solely concerning repairs and maintenance of existing 
facilities and is not of a sufficient magnitude that a prudent person would find it foreseeable that the 
decision would result in a· change of value for either the trust as a business entity or the mortgage 
firm that is currently leasing the property. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
Sincerely, 
By: 
Counsel, Legal Division 
SKP:jgl 
5 See Regulation.18702.3{a){4).

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Under the Act: may you participate in the City Council's decisions concerning the removal 
or maintenance of the trees lining Broadway, given that your family trust owns property on a 
neighboring block and receives lease payments from the mortgage firm that leases the property?

CONCLUSION: Yes. You may participate in those decisions because they would not result in a reasonably 
foreseeable material financial effect on any of your interests based on the facts presented.
